blockingIssues.description: Shell command injection in deploy execution: `execSync` at src/cli.ts:2125 interpolates env-derived FLY_APP_NAME, FLY_PRIMARY_REGION, and flyctlPath into a shell command string without escaping or using an argument-vector API. Crafted env values can execute arbitrary commands. Fix: use execFileSync or spawnSync with argv array instead of shell string interpolation.

Shell command injection in preflight auth check: `execSync` at src/deploy.ts:190 interpolates flyctlPath (which may come from MORS_DEPLOY_FLYCTL_PATH env var) into a shell command string. Same risk as above. Fix: use execFileSync(flyctlPath, ['auth', 'whoami'], ...) instead.

VAL-CROSS-008 restart/deploy integrity tests at test/cross/golden-path.test.ts:1302 reuse the same in-memory RelayMessageStore instance across simulated restarts. This tests server re-creation but not true process-level restart with store rehydration. The tests can pass even if restart actually loses state. Fix: use a persistence-backed store or at minimum create a new store instance initialized from serialized state to validate restart boundary.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Workers must never use shell string interpolation with env-derived or user-derived values in execSync/child_process calls. Use execFileSync or spawnSync with argument-vector arrays for all subprocess invocations involving dynamic values.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature fly-deploy-artifacts-and-safe-placeholder-failures introduced shell-string execSync at src/cli.ts:2125 and src/deploy.ts:190 using env-derived FLY_APP_NAME, FLY_PRIMARY_REGION, and flyctlPath without escaping. This was not caught by existing conventions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify mors-global-worker skill procedure for regression-guard and test-only features: (1) strict red/green TDD may not apply when adding tests for already-correct behavior — allow explicit carve-out with rationale; (2) require manual-check entries or explicit skip rationale in handoff for features that add user-facing commands or significant test coverage.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three workers in this milestone all reported followedProcedure=true despite: (a) launch-path regression tests passing immediately on first run (no red phase), (b) deploy feature adding user-facing CLI command without manual-check evidence, (c) cross-golden-path feature having no interactiveChecks entries. Pattern is systemic across all three workers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add knowledge note: 'VAL-CROSS-008 restart/deploy integrity tests currently use in-memory store simulation, not true process restart. Workers addressing this must use persistence-backed stores or serialized-state rehydration to validate restart boundary behavior.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: test/cross/golden-path.test.ts:1302 explicitly documents 'Shared message store survives restart — simulates persistent storage' but this simulation does not exercise the real rehydration path. Future workers need to know this limitation exists.

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill-procedure gap for strict red/green TDD on regression-guard features (from install-ux reviewer)
rejectedObservations.reason: Duplicate — already captured as systemic suggestion above covering all three features

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill-procedure manual-check requirement clarity (from cross-golden-path reviewer)
rejectedObservations.reason: Duplicate — merged into systemic skills suggestion above
